description: Foundation systems: Elemental System, Status Effects, Extended GameEventType, Extended GameState, and modifications to GameCharacter to support elements and status. These are the building blocks that all other features depend on.
steps: Create src/model/skills/Element.java enum with FIRE, HOLY, DARK, PHYSICAL, POISON. Include static method getMultiplier(Element attackElement, Element defenseElement) with the multiplier table: FIRE vs DARK=2.0, HOLY vs DARK=2.0, POISON vs PHYSICAL=1.5, DARK vs HOLY=1.5, same element=0.5, neutral=1.0.
Create src/model/status/StatusType.java enum: POISON, REGEN, ENRAGE, SHIELD, CURSE, STUN.

Create src/model/status/StatusEffect.java class with: StatusType type, int duration, int potency, String sourceName. Methods: tick() decrements duration and returns effect value, isExpired() returns duration <= 0. Extends GameEventDispatcher to fire STATUS_TICKED events.

Create src/model/status/StatusManager.java class extending GameEventDispatcher. Fields: List<StatusEffect> activeEffects. Methods: addEffect(StatusEffect) fires STATUS_APPLIED event, tickAll() processes all effects returning list of GameEvents (poison damage, regen heal, etc.) and fires STATUS_TICKED/STATUS_EXPIRED events, hasEffect(StatusType), removeEffect(StatusType), isStunned() returns hasEffect(STUN). Poison is stackable up to 3 stacks. SHIELD blocks next damage then expires.

Extend GameEventType.java to add all new event types: ITEM_EQUIPPED, ITEM_UNEQUIPPED, ITEM_DROPPED, STATUS_APPLIED, STATUS_TICKED, STATUS_EXPIRED, ENEMY_TELEGRAPH, ENEMY_ABILITY_FIRED, SKILL_UNLOCKED, SKILL_CHOICE_OFFERED, SKILL_ON_COOLDOWN, EVENT_ROOM_ENTERED, EVENT_ROOM_CHOICE_MADE, CURSE_APPLIED, RUN_MODIFIER_APPLIED, SHIELD_BLOCKED, SHIELD_BROKEN, ENEMY_FORMATION_SPAWNED.
Extend GameState.java to add: SKILL_CHOICE, EVENT_ROOM, EQUIP_SCREEN.

Modify GameCharacter.java to: (a) add a StatusManager field initialized in constructor, (b) add getStatusManager() method, (c) add overloaded takeDamage(int damage, Element attackElement) method that applies element multiplier using Element.getMultiplier() against the character's element, then delegates to existing takeDamage logic, (d) add an 'element' field (Element, defaults to PHYSICAL) with getter and protected setter, (e) in the existing takeDamage(int) method, check if StatusManager has SHIELD - if so, block damage completely, fire SHIELD_BLOCKED event, remove shield, fire SHIELD_BROKEN event, and return without dealing damage.

Modify Enemy.java to accept Element in constructors. Update existing enemy subclasses: Goblin gets Element.PHYSICAL, Skeleton gets Element.DARK, GoblinKing gets Element.PHYSICAL, Lich gets Element.DARK.
Update Player.java default element to PHYSICAL.

Ensure all new files have proper package declarations and imports. Verify compilation with: javac -d out -sourcepath src src/model/events/*.java src/model/status/*.java src/model/skills/*.java src/model/*.java src/controller/*.java
acceptance_criteria: All files compile cleanly with javac
Element.getMultiplier(FIRE, DARK) returns 2.0, Element.getMultiplier(HOLY, DARK) returns 2.0, Element.getMultiplier(FIRE, FIRE) returns 0.5, Element.getMultiplier(FIRE, PHYSICAL) returns 1.0
StatusManager can add, tick, and expire effects correctly
GameCharacter.takeDamage(damage, element) applies multiplier before defense formula
SHIELD status blocks damage completely and then expires
GameEventType has all 18 new event types
No System.in/out/err in model or controller packages
verification: Run: javac -d out -sourcepath src src/model/events/*.java src/model/status/*.java src/model/skills/*.java src/model/*.java src/controller/*.java - must exit with code 0 and no errors
Run: grep -rn 'System\.' src/model/ src/controller/ | grep -v '// ' - should return no matches for System.in/out/err
findings: All files compile cleanly with javac. No System.in/out/err found in model or controller. The build command from context.json works as-is (includes status/ and skills/ globs). Enemy subclasses now use the overloaded constructor with Element parameter. GameCharacter defaults element to PHYSICAL in base constructor, so the Player setElement call is technically redundant but explicit for clarity. Review fix pass applied: SHIELD now uses counter-based blocking (decrementShield) to support multi-hit shields like Divine Shield (2 hits).
